Template 3 — Carousel (4 slides max) Mockup · Sample copy

1080×1080 square × 4 slides · Default OFF — only for special features (project recap, multi-step guide) · For sign-off only — copy is placeholder

Slide 1 — Title
{SERIES_KICKER}

{SERIES_TITLE} A title that sets up the story.

Blick.
Swipe →
Slide 2 — Idea
01

{POINT_HEADLINE_1} First idea, sentence case, single thought.

{POINT_BODY_1} A short supporting paragraph, two or three lines, plain English.

2 / 4
Swipe →
Slide 3 — Idea
02

{POINT_HEADLINE_2} Second idea, builds on slide 2.

{POINT_BODY_2} Same length and rhythm as slide 2 so the carousel reads consistent.

3 / 4
Swipe →
Slide 4 — Closer
{CLOSER_KICKER}

{CLOSER_HEADLINE} The takeaway, or the call to action.

Blick.
www.blick.group

Template 3 — Carousel (special features only)

This template is OFF by default. Used only when a single post is too small for the story (e.g. a multi-step troubleshooting guide, a recap of a project, a deeper teaching piece). Most weeks: skip the carousel entirely.

Structure

  • Slide 1 (ink) — title, sets the topic
  • Slide 2 (warm) — first idea
  • Slide 3 (white) — second idea
  • Slide 4 (ink) — closer, takeaway or CTA

Hard rules

  • 4 slides max. If you need 5, the story is two posts.
  • One topic per carousel. Never mix unrelated subjects (the CCL carousel mistake).
  • Canvas alternates ink → warm → white → ink. Visual rhythm pulls the reader through.
  • Brand orange is the kicker, the rule, and the swipe cue. No fourth use.

Replaceable fields

  • {SERIES_KICKER} · {SERIES_TITLE}
  • {POINT_HEADLINE_1..2} · {POINT_BODY_1..2}
  • {CLOSER_KICKER} · {CLOSER_HEADLINE}
